title: Securing Agentic Architectures: A Deliberately Vulnerable Multi-Agent LLM Testbed
description: Experimental prototype accompanying the master's thesis "Securing Agentic Architectures" (TU Wien). A deliberately vulnerable three-agent LLM system with seven attack scenarios (memory poisoning, indirect injection, cross-agent propagation, planner hijacking, system prompt extraction, memory membership probing, and tool result poisoning) and five defences (canary tokens, intent anchoring, counterfactual plan diffing, output spotlighting, and a provenance-based trust hierarchy), evaluated across fourteen models: six paid API models across three vendors (OpenAI GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini, Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Claude Haiku 4.5, and DeepSeek-V3 and DeepSeek-R1) and eight free local open-weight models (Qwen2.5 in five sizes, Llama 3.2 1B/3B, Llama 3.1 8B). Includes the full experiment runner and the benchmark result files used to produce the thesis tables.
